title: Full Disclosure

jokes.text: I have a black belt in Mike Kwan Do
jokes.setup: Mike claims he can 'bust some moves' in town, suggesting political savvy
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually a martial arts pun on his own name
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Mike - self-aggrandizing but ultimately revealing his dorky nature. Clean wordplay with good economy.

jokes.text: Hands off the suit
jokes.setup: Mike just made a martial arts boast
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflates when physically confronted, protecting his suit instead of fighting
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that undercuts Mike's bravado, showing his priorities are appearance over action.

jokes.text: You must have a huge schadenboner over this Amy flack
jokes.setup: Dan calling about Amy's public humiliation
jokes.punchline: Combines 'schadenfreude' with 'boner' to create a vulgar portmanteau about taking pleasure in others' pain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant neologism that captures both Dan's character and the show's linguistic creativity. Economical and memorable.

jokes.text: God, she is gonna go ape-shit menstrual over this
jokes.setup: Dan describing Amy's potential reaction to the news
jokes.punchline: Uses 'menstrual' as an intensifier, creating uncomfortable gender-based humor
jokes.explanation: Typical Veep vulgarity that reveals character callousness, though somewhat lazy gender-based humor.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm on the phone with AT&T. Can you stop shouting 'menstrual'?
jokes.setup: Dan keeps saying 'menstrual' loudly
jokes.punchline: Jonah's mundane phone call interrupted by inappropriate shouting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the menstrual bit, showing how the office dysfunction affects even mundane tasks.

jokes.text: like the contents of a broken fucking condom
jokes.setup: Dan describing how rumors are flying around
jokes.punchline: Vivid, crude metaphor for uncontrolled spread
jokes.explanation: Excellent crude metaphor that's both vivid and thematically relevant given the pregnancy subplot.

jokes.text: This is going in with the dirty dishes, man. For fuck's sake. If this is broken, I'm adding it to your fucking rent
jokes.setup: Background domestic chaos during important phone call
jokes.punchline: Mundane roommate concerns interrupting political crisis
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of high-stakes political talk with petty roommate drama, showing character priorities.

jokes.text: Well, I was gonna say that brings a smile to my face, but that would be unsafe, wouldn't it, ma'am?
jokes.setup: Previous context about Secret Service agent being reassigned for smiling
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about the absurdity of the situation
jokes.explanation: Clever self-referential humor that acknowledges the absurdity of the central premise.

jokes.text: That's very funny, Bill. You're fired. I'm kidding. I'm kidding, I'm kidding
jokes.setup: Bill makes a joke about the firing situation
jokes.punchline: Selina pretends to fire him for joking, then immediately backtracks
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Selina's power anxiety and her tendency to overreact then retreat.

jokes.text: 'Secret' as in shut the fuck up. And 'service' as in you work for me
jokes.setup: Selina angry about Secret Service leak
jokes.punchline: Breaks down 'Secret Service' into aggressive commands
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that shows Selina's linguistic creativity when angry. Clean structure and good character voice.

jokes.text: I want a name and a severed head that answers to that name, or would if it could still talk
jokes.setup: Selina demanding consequences for the leak
jokes.punchline: Extends violent metaphor to absurd logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that escalates a common metaphor to absurdist heights, showing Selina's verbal creativity under pressure.

jokes.text: Ooh, Gary, did the veep just bitch-slap you with a door?
jokes.setup: Selina slammed her office door, presumably hitting Gary
jokes.punchline: Dan's cruel observation of Gary's physical and emotional pain
jokes.explanation: Classic Dan cruelty, capitalizing on others' misfortune. Good visual gag inference and character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, what, is she out of hand sanitizer again?
jokes.setup: Gary says he can't tell them something important
jokes.punchline: Dan dismisses it as another mundane Gary concern
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing how the staff views Gary's typical concerns as trivial.

jokes.text: Gary, why are you talking in cliffhangers?
jokes.setup: Gary being evasive about important information
jokes.punchline: Amy's perfect description of his dramatic hesitation
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor that perfectly captures Gary's dramatic tendencies in TV terminology.

jokes.text: Well, this is good for us. Nicely done, Dandroid
jokes.setup: Learning about Selina's miscarriage
jokes.punchline: Mike's immediate political calculation and Amy's robotic nickname for Dan
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing the characters' immediate pivot to political advantage, plus a good nickname for Dan.

jokes.text: Free from a major fucking political headache, I'd say. That's deep space cold, Dan. Explains why you piss liquid nitrogen
jokes.setup: Dan's callous response to the miscarriage news
jokes.punchline: Mike's vivid metaphor for Dan's coldness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating metaphor from 'deep space cold' to the liquid nitrogen punchline. Creative and character-revealing.

jokes.text: Uh, I was telling the guys about my riding mower I got
jokes.setup: Gary clearly just revealed something shocking about Selina
jokes.punchline: Pathetic attempt to cover with mundane lawn equipment story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - terrible at covering his tracks with the most mundane possible deflection.

jokes.text: White House is in the house. * Everybody say way-o! *
jokes.setup: White House staff arriving for serious meeting
jokes.punchline: Jonah's inappropriate party announcement
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah inappropriateness - completely tone-deaf announcement for a crisis situation.

jokes.text: The skyscraper of shit has arrived
jokes.setup: White House representatives arriving with bad news
jokes.punchline: Amy's architectural metaphor for their problems
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor that captures both the scale and nature of their incoming problems with economy.

jokes.text: It's tagged 'Selina Sell-Outta'
jokes.setup: Describing a viral video about Selina
jokes.punchline: Hashtag that plays on her name with 'sellout'
jokes.explanation: Decent political wordplay, though feels like something that would actually happen in politics.

jokes.text: I am Selina Meyer. And I don't have any idea what's going on in my office
jokes.setup: Viral video mocking Selina
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment delivered as if by Selina herself
jokes.explanation: Effective political satire that hits at the core criticism of the character - competence.

jokes.text: All right, pack up your limbs and get the fuck out, Jonah
jokes.setup: Selina angry at Jonah for showing the video
jokes.punchline: Bizarre instruction to 'pack up your limbs' instead of normal dismissal
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd variation on 'pack your things and leave' that's both funny and memorably strange.

jokes.text: I have a much longer reach
jokes.setup: Someone asks if punching is allowed
jokes.punchline: Jonah's confidence based purely on his height advantage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah - reducing fighting prowess to pure physical measurement, showing his simple-minded approach to conflict.

jokes.text: And technically this is a fully functioning office, too
jokes.setup: Amy pointing out technical accuracies in the video
jokes.punchline: Selina sarcastically includes their dysfunctional office in the 'technically true' list
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor that acknowledges the office's dysfunction while maintaining the 'technically' pattern.

jokes.text: You are the closest to normal we've got going in this office right now
jokes.setup: Selina needs someone to handle the Secret Service situation
jokes.punchline: Mike is their 'most normal' option, which is damning
jokes.explanation: Great backhanded compliment that reveals how dysfunctional everyone else is if Mike is the normal one.

jokes.text: I don't read half the stuff I'm supposed to
jokes.setup: Explaining that people won't read all their released documents
jokes.punchline: Selina admits she doesn't even read her own required materials
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Selina's casual admission of her own professional negligence.

jokes.text: Just because this isn't your baby, you don't... God, I am so sorry
jokes.setup: Dan criticizing the plan using 'baby' metaphor, forgetting about the miscarriage
jokes.punchline: Immediate horrified realization of the poor word choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where casual metaphor becomes deeply inappropriate given recent events.

jokes.text: I mean, it was like a heavy period. Don't worry about it
jokes.setup: Dan's mortified apology for the baby reference
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutal minimization of her miscarriage
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant moment showing Selina's coping mechanism and political instincts overriding personal trauma. Economical and shocking.

jokes.text: Why would you be writing that down?
jokes.setup: Mike listing things they need to hide from full disclosure
jokes.punchline: Realizes someone is creating the exact paper trail they're trying to avoid
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the cover-up incompetence, showing how they create their own problems.

jokes.text: So it doesn't look as if we've just airbrushed the nipples out of this fucking thing
jokes.setup: Explaining why they need some embarrassing content in the release
jokes.punchline: Compares document sanitization to airbrushing nude photos
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that perfectly captures the idea of making something too clean to be believable.

jokes.text: Your back facials. Oh. Back facials? What? Yeah, I get back acne. Backne
jokes.setup: Discussing embarrassing details to include in the release
jokes.punchline: Selina's mortifying personal grooming revelation and portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing the embarrassing personal details that become political fodder.

jokes.text: I do know that I can't know what you know or you don't know. You know?
jokes.setup: Selina trying to maintain plausible deniability about Dan's activities
jokes.punchline: Verbal pretzel of knowledge and ignorance
jokes.explanation: Decent verbal confusion joke showing political double-speak, though somewhat convoluted.

jokes.text: The nod of my head is like I'm buffering
jokes.setup: Selina asking what Amy's nodding means
jokes.punchline: Amy compares her thinking process to computer loading
jokes.explanation: Good tech metaphor for human thinking that feels natural and character-appropriate for Amy.

jokes.text: You've got to put black marker over all the words
jokes.setup: Mike checking document redactions
jokes.punchline: Instruction to redact everything, defeating the purpose entirely
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their transparency incompetence, showing how they defeat their own purpose.

jokes.text: I know, who'd have thought transparent government would take up so much of your field of vision?
jokes.setup: Press conference about document release
jokes.punchline: Visual pun on 'transparency' and 'field of vision'
jokes.explanation: Decent visual pun, though feels slightly forced. Shows Selina trying to be clever under pressure.

jokes.text: Yes, sir. Went to Iraq with the veep. Presented a Thanksgiving turkey. I was there. No fire as I recall. No, but the line was there
jokes.setup: Agent asking if Mike has been 'in the line of fire'
jokes.punchline: Mike's pathetic attempt to claim combat experience from a ceremonial turkey presentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - trying to claim military credibility from the most civilian possible experience.

jokes.text: Does he ever say anything funny?
jokes.setup: Learning the agent cares for uncle with dementia
jokes.punchline: Mike's inappropriate attempt to find humor in dementia
jokes.explanation: Shows Mike's inappropriate social instincts, though relies on cheap shock value rather than clever construction.

jokes.text: Security guys are supposed to be like wallpaper. They're not supposed to be grinning like some ape
jokes.setup: Mike trying to justify the agent's reassignment
jokes.punchline: Doubles down with an even more offensive comparison
jokes.explanation: Shows Mike's tendency to make bad situations worse, though the ape comparison is fairly lazy humor.

jokes.text: They're calling it an e-nami
jokes.setup: Press describing their document dump strategy
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'email' and 'tsunami'
jokes.explanation: Decent media portmanteau that feels like real political coverage, though not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Hey, gimme a 'J'!
jokes.setup: Jonah entering during a crisis meeting
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate cheerleader enthusiasm during serious discussion
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah tone-deafness, though the cheerleader bit is somewhat predictable for his character.

jokes.text: A cake in the shape of a dick. A smart new hat in the shape of a dick
jokes.setup: Reading birthday gift suggestions from leaked emails
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurdity of dick-shaped gift suggestions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, though relies on juvenile humor. The 'smart new hat' addition makes it more absurd.

jokes.text: And they are whispering 'Dick Cake'
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about people recognizing him from the leaked email
jokes.punchline: His mortification at being known as 'Dick Cake'
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the earlier dick cake setup, showing consequences of the leak in personal terms for Jonah.

jokes.text: Uh, Dick Cake
jokes.setup: Amy asking who DC refers to in their system
jokes.punchline: Ryan's matter-of-fact confirmation of Jonah's embarrassing nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that makes the nickname official. Great economy in just two words.

jokes.text: Mike, can't you make me not have been pregnant?
jokes.setup: Complications from the pregnancy rumors and overnight visits
jokes.punchline: Selina's desperate request to retroactively change reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment showing Selina's complete desperation and magical thinking about spin.

jokes.text: This is what happens when you tell the truth. Nothing good comes of it
jokes.setup: Their transparency initiative backfiring spectacularly
jokes.punchline: Mike's cynical but accurate assessment of honesty in politics
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary delivered with perfect cynicism. Captures the show's view of political honesty.

jokes.text: This is gonna cause a total potal meltdown
jokes.setup: Describing the President's likely reaction
jokes.punchline: Rhyming abbreviation for 'President of the United States'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Mike's tendency to create unnecessary abbreviations and rhymes.

jokes.text: Yeah, why don't you run along and see if you can suck your own cake?
jokes.setup: Dan dismissing Jonah after the dick cake revelation
jokes.punchline: Turns 'suck your own dick' into cake-based insult
jokes.explanation: Clever transformation of a crude insult using the established cake motif. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: Remind me, who did you blackmail to get this job?
jokes.setup: Mike's bungled handling of the Secret Service situation
jokes.punchline: Amy's sarcastic question about his qualifications
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Amy's frustration with Mike's incompetence in a memorable way.

jokes.text: Hey, suck-up isn't gonna fix a fuck-up, Dan
jokes.setup: Dan trying to blame others for the crisis
jokes.punchline: Selina's rhyming dismissal of his attempt
jokes.explanation: Great rhyming put-down that perfectly captures Selina's frustration with Dan's political maneuvering.

jokes.text: I'll give you a fucking deal on all three of them, too, if you want
jokes.setup: Ted complaining about not having staff support
jokes.punchline: Selina offering to sell her entire team like used cars
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Selina's complete disillusionment with her staff, treating them as disposable commodities.

jokes.text: So why don't you just take your beak and shove it up some corpse's ass, okay, you vulture motherfucker?
jokes.setup: Selina angry at persistent journalist
jokes.punchline: Elaborate vulture metaphor with escalating vulgarity
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor showing Selina's verbal creativity when truly enraged. Strong visual imagery.

jokes.text: You can't tell him to fuck his mother. Her mother, actually
jokes.setup: Ted criticizing Selina's vulgar outburst
jokes.punchline: Selina's pedantic gender correction while missing the bigger point
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's tendency to focus on irrelevant details when criticized.

jokes.text: I'm gonna be the dumper. He's gonna be the dumpee
jokes.setup: Selina explaining why Gary needs to break up with Ted for her
jokes.punchline: Reducing relationship dynamics to simple dumper/dumpee categories
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's need to control narratives even in personal relationships.

jokes.text: I don't want to end up with a horse's head in my bed. Though there's room for one now
jokes.setup: Selina needing to meet with gaming association (Godfather reference)
jokes.punchline: Darkly references her now-empty bed after Ted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor connecting Godfather reference to her personal situation. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Wait a second. Did you just call me buddy?
jokes.setup: Dan being unusually nice to Gary
jokes.punchline: Gary immediately suspicious of uncharacteristic kindness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how unusual kindness is in this office that it's immediately suspicious.

jokes.text: Let's pretend there's a female farmer and she's got a horse, a sheep, and a pig, okay? The pig
jokes.setup: Dan creating elaborate metaphor to get information about firings
jokes.punchline: Gary's immediate answer reveals the plan
jokes.explanation: Clever setup showing Dan's manipulative skills and Gary's inability to keep secrets even in metaphor.

jokes.text: I'd rather be a pig than an ass-monkey up on his high horse
jokes.setup: Continuation of the animal metaphor conversation
jokes.punchline: Gary mixing multiple animal metaphors in defiant response
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the animal metaphor with Gary showing unexpected wit in combining multiple animal references.

jokes.text: Did you just sidle?
jokes.setup: Dan approaching Amy in a sneaky manner
jokes.punchline: Amy's precise identification of his movement style
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor with perfect economy. Amy's precision with language is character-perfect.

jokes.text: One condition... I watch you die first and I get to stab you repeatedly to make sure.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to the suicide pact
jokes.punchline: Adding sadistic conditions to the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan - even in cooperation, he's finding ways to be cruel and self-serving.

jokes.text: Not with testicles
jokes.setup: Mike saying 'three is better than two'
jokes.punchline: Amy's blunt anatomical correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with maximum economy. Amy's directness cutting through meaningless platitude.

jokes.text: Do you mean un-redacting? Taking the black off
jokes.setup: Mike claiming to be good at 'redacting' (putting black on)
jokes.punchline: Amy correcting his backwards understanding, Mike's simple explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mike's fundamental misunderstanding of his own claimed expertise.

jokes.text: Oh, we got fired
jokes.setup: Explaining what happened with his previous suicide pact
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that their strategy completely failed
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of their master plan with Mike's casual delivery revealing total failure.

jokes.text: What's her shoe size? Seven. Seven and a half.
jokes.setup: Gary and Sue competing over who knows Selina better
jokes.punchline: Sue winning with more precise shoe size knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing the absurd level of detail in their competition.

jokes.text: Like when you bought pregnancy tests for the veep wearing your staff ID?
jokes.setup: Gary claiming to be infallible about Selina knowledge
jokes.punchline: Sue's devastating revelation of his major security breach
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that completely undercuts Gary's smugness while revealing the source of their problems.

jokes.text: High-pathetic.
jokes.setup: Mike calling the suicide pact 'hypothetical'
jokes.punchline: Selina's cruel pun on 'hypothetical'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining 'hypothetical' with 'pathetic.' Very Selina wit.

jokes.text: Well, equally, 'cause it's been my life
jokes.setup: Gary saying he's loved the job more than life itself
jokes.punchline: Immediate clarification revealing his complete lack of outside life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment revealing his complete devotion and lack of personal life outside Selina.

jokes.text: we say some of us mishandled things because you were pregnant? I mean... no, no. No, stupid idea.
jokes.setup: Amy desperately pitching ideas to save herself
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing how terrible her own suggestion is
jokes.explanation: Good desperation moment. Amy's brain working and rejecting ideas in real time.

jokes.text: Could say the pregnancy test was for me
jokes.setup: Amy desperately trying to find solutions to their crisis
jokes.punchline: Offering to sacrifice her own reputation with fake pregnancy story
jokes.explanation: Shows Amy's complete desperation and willingness to sacrifice herself for the team, revealing character depth.

jokes.text: Knockity knock knock knock. Peep you later
jokes.setup: Mike interrupting serious interview with muffin offer
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate casual language and timing
jokes.explanation: Classic Mike tone-deafness, though the 'peep you later' feels slightly forced even for his character.

jokes.text: From the place that we like eight blocks away
jokes.setup: Dan offering to get iced tea during fake interview
jokes.punchline: Selina making the errand unnecessarily difficult as punishment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's petty revenge and how she punishes staff through inconvenience.

jokes.text: Is he the father? Oh, no, no. Father, no. He can't
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about Dan's relationship to Amy's fake pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Amy's flustered denial implying Dan's impotence/sterility
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake interview going wrong, with Amy accidentally creating new problems while solving others.

tmdb_overview: The vice president's office takes some heat over a pregnancy rumor and the firing of a Secret Service agent, so Selina establishes some new ground rules about internal correspondence. Meanwhile, Dan tries to cover his boss' tracks on the clean-jobs bill; and a staffer's job may be in jeopardy.
insight: Escalation-driven finale hits 71 jokes in 31 minutes but falls short of elite tier.
